---
title: "Notebook 06 — GARCH Volatility Estimation and OOS Forecasting"
author: "Sourodeep Roy"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
    theme: flatly
---

```{r nb06_setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(rugarch)
library(sandwich)   # NeweyWest
library(lmtest)     # coeftest

# ── Anchor all paths to project root ────────────────────────────────────────
proj_root <- rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file()

# ── Load 5-min objects produced by notebook 05 ──────────────────────────────
r5_apr <- readRDS(file.path(proj_root, "data/processed/r5_apr.rds"))
r5_may <- readRDS(file.path(proj_root, "data/processed/r5_may.rds"))
r5_jun <- readRDS(file.path(proj_root, "data/processed/r5_jun.rds"))

# Confirm required columns
req_cols <- c("r_raw_5m", "r_ia_5m_sum", "r_ia_5m_inv", "psi_5m", "scale_5m")
stopifnot(all(req_cols %in% names(r5_apr)),
          all(req_cols %in% names(r5_may)),
          all(req_cols %in% names(r5_jun)))

cat("Project root:", proj_root, "\n")
cat("Rows: Apr =", nrow(r5_apr), "| May =", nrow(r5_may), "| Jun =", nrow(r5_jun), "\n")
```

```{r nb06_series_setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ── VOLATILITY TRACK (for GARCH fitting + forecast evaluation) ───────────────
# r_ia_5m_sum: sum of event-level IA returns per 5-min bucket.
# This is the denoised series whose variance GARCH should model more cleanly
# after activity-driven heteroskedasticity has been removed by the ACD scaling.

r_apr_raw_vol <- as.numeric(r5_apr$r_raw_5m)
r_apr_ia_vol  <- as.numeric(r5_apr$r_ia_5m_sum)

r_may_raw_vol <- as.numeric(r5_may$r_raw_5m)
r_may_ia_vol  <- as.numeric(r5_may$r_ia_5m_sum)

r_jun_raw_vol <- as.numeric(r5_jun$r_raw_5m)
r_jun_ia_vol  <- as.numeric(r5_jun$r_ia_5m_sum)

stopifnot(
  all(is.finite(r_apr_raw_vol)), all(is.finite(r_apr_ia_vol)),
  all(is.finite(r_may_raw_vol)), all(is.finite(r_may_ia_vol)),
  all(is.finite(r_jun_raw_vol)), all(is.finite(r_jun_ia_vol))
)

cat("Volatility track lengths:\n")
cat("Apr raw:", length(r_apr_raw_vol), "| Apr IA-sum:", length(r_apr_ia_vol), "\n")
cat("May raw:", length(r_may_raw_vol), "| May IA-sum:", length(r_may_ia_vol), "\n")
cat("Jun raw:", length(r_jun_raw_vol), "| Jun IA-sum:", length(r_jun_ia_vol), "\n")
```

```{r nb06_clustering_diagnostics, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ── DIAGNOSTIC 1: ACF of squared returns ─────────────────────────────────────
# Significant positive autocorrelation in r² is the empirical signature of
# GARCH effects. We expect IA-sum to show weaker but still present clustering
# after the activity adjustment — the residual clustering is what GARCH models.

op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
acf(r_apr_raw_vol^2, lag.max = 100,
    main = "April ACF(r²) — Raw, 5-min")
acf(r_apr_ia_vol^2,  lag.max = 100,
    main = "April ACF(r²) — IA-sum, 5-min")
par(op)

# ── DIAGNOSTIC 2: ARCH-LM test (Engle 1982) ──────────────────────────────────
# Standard pre-GARCH check. Ljung-Box on squared returns at multiple lags.
# Rejection confirms ARCH effects are present and justifies GARCH modelling.

arch_lm <- function(r, lags = c(5, 10, 20)) {
  r2 <- as.numeric(r)^2
  rbindlist(lapply(lags, function(l) {
    bt <- Box.test(r2, lag = l, type = "Ljung-Box")
    data.table(lag = l, stat = round(bt$statistic, 3), p_value = round(bt$p.value, 6))
  }))
}

cat("ARCH-LM (Ljung-Box on r²) — April RAW:\n")
print(arch_lm(r_apr_raw_vol))
cat("\nARCH-LM (Ljung-Box on r²) — April IA-sum:\n")
print(arch_lm(r_apr_ia_vol))

# ── DIAGNOSTIC 3: Absolute returns time-series ───────────────────────────────
dt_abs <- rbindlist(list(
  data.table(t = seq_along(r_apr_raw_vol), abs_r = abs(r_apr_raw_vol), Series = "Raw"),
  data.table(t = seq_along(r_apr_ia_vol),  abs_r = abs(r_apr_ia_vol),  Series = "IA-sum")
))

ggplot(dt_abs, aes(x = t, y = abs_r)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~Series, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "April (5-min): absolute returns — volatility clustering",
       x = "5-min bucket index", y = "|r|") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r nb06_model_selection, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ── MODEL SELECTION: compare three standard GARCH specifications ──────────────
# Practitioners and researchers routinely compare sGARCH, eGARCH, and GJR-GARCH
# to account for asymmetric leverage effects (negative returns increase vol more
# than positive returns of equal magnitude). Student-t innovations are the
# baseline for high-frequency financial returns given heavy tails.
# Selection criterion: BIC (penalises parameters more aggressively than AIC).

garch_specs <- list(
  sGARCH_std   = ugarchspec(
    variance.model     = list(model = "sGARCH",   garchOrder = c(1, 1)),
    mean.model         = list(armaOrder = c(0, 0), include.mean = FALSE),
    distribution.model = "std"),
  eGARCH_std   = ugarchspec(
    variance.model     = list(model = "eGARCH",   garchOrder = c(1, 1)),
    mean.model         = list(armaOrder = c(0, 0), include.mean = FALSE),
    distribution.model = "std"),
  gjrGARCH_std = ugarchspec(
    variance.model     = list(model = "gjrGARCH", garchOrder = c(1, 1)),
    mean.model         = list(armaOrder = c(0, 0), include.mean = FALSE),
    distribution.model = "std")
)

fit_and_ic <- function(spec, data, label) {
  fit <- ugarchfit(spec, data = data, solver = "hybrid")
  if (fit@fit$convergence != 0) {
    warning("Non-convergence: ", label)
    return(NULL)
  }
  ic <- infocriteria(fit)
  list(label = label, fit = fit,
       AIC = ic["Akaike", ], BIC = ic["Bayes", ])
}

# Fit all three specs on April RAW
res_raw <- lapply(seq_along(garch_specs), function(i)
  fit_and_ic(garch_specs[[i]], r_apr_raw_vol, names(garch_specs)[i]))
res_raw <- Filter(Negate(is.null), res_raw)

# Fit all three specs on April IA-sum
res_ia <- lapply(seq_along(garch_specs), function(i)
  fit_and_ic(garch_specs[[i]], r_apr_ia_vol, names(garch_specs)[i]))
res_ia <- Filter(Negate(is.null), res_ia)

# Information criteria table
ic_raw <- rbindlist(lapply(res_raw, function(x)
  data.table(Track = "Raw",    Model = x$label,
             AIC = round(x$AIC, 4), BIC = round(x$BIC, 4))))
ic_ia  <- rbindlist(lapply(res_ia, function(x)
  data.table(Track = "IA-sum", Model = x$label,
             AIC = round(x$AIC, 4), BIC = round(x$BIC, 4))))

ic_table <- rbindlist(list(ic_raw, ic_ia))
setorder(ic_table, Track, BIC)
cat("Model selection (April training; lower BIC preferred):\n")
print(ic_table)

# Select best spec per track by BIC
best_raw <- res_raw[[which.min(sapply(res_raw, `[[`, "BIC"))]]
best_ia  <- res_ia [[which.min(sapply(res_ia,  `[[`, "BIC"))]]

cat("\nSelected: RAW =>", best_raw$label, "| IA-sum =>", best_ia$label, "\n")

spec_raw_best <- garch_specs[[best_raw$label]]
spec_ia_best  <- garch_specs[[best_ia$label]]
fit_raw_vol   <- best_raw$fit
fit_ia_vol    <- best_ia$fit

cat("\nRAW fit coefficients:\n");    print(round(coef(fit_raw_vol), 6))
cat("\nIA-sum fit coefficients:\n"); print(round(coef(fit_ia_vol),  6))
```

```{r nb06_oos_sigma, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ── OOS SIGMA: freeze April parameters, filter May/Jun ───────────────────────
# Correct approach: fit only on April, lock parameters with setfixed(), then
# use ugarchfilter() to propagate the variance equation through OOS months.
# This strictly prevents any look-ahead leakage and mirrors what a practitioner
# would do at month-end: freeze the model, run it forward on new data.

oos_sigma_fixed <- function(fit_obj, r_oos) {
  r_oos <- as.numeric(r_oos)
  stopifnot(all(is.finite(r_oos)), length(r_oos) > 0)

  spec_fx <- getspec(fit_obj)
  setfixed(spec_fx) <- as.list(coef(fit_obj))

  flt <- ugarchfilter(spec_fx, data = r_oos)
  sig <- as.numeric(sigma(flt))

  stopifnot(all(is.finite(sig)), all(sig > 0), length(sig) == length(r_oos))
  sig
}

# May
sigma_raw_may <- oos_sigma_fixed(fit_raw_vol, r_may_raw_vol)
sigma_ia_may  <- oos_sigma_fixed(fit_ia_vol,  r_may_ia_vol)

# Jun
sigma_raw_jun <- oos_sigma_fixed(fit_raw_vol, r_jun_raw_vol)
sigma_ia_jun  <- oos_sigma_fixed(fit_ia_vol,  r_jun_ia_vol)

cat("OOS sigma lengths:\n")
cat("May: raw =", length(sigma_raw_may), "| ia =", length(sigma_ia_may), "\n")
cat("Jun: raw =", length(sigma_raw_jun), "| ia =", length(sigma_ia_jun), "\n")

# ── Visual sanity: smoothed r² vs σ² (May, 4-hour rolling window) ─────────────
k <- 48  # 48 × 5 min = 4 hours

dt_ck <- data.table(
  t      = seq_along(r_may_raw_vol),
  rv_raw = r_may_raw_vol^2,
  v_raw  = sigma_raw_may^2,
  rv_ia  = r_may_ia_vol^2,
  v_ia   = sigma_ia_may^2
)
dt_ck[, rv_raw_s := zoo::rollmean(rv_raw, k = k, fill = NA, align = "right")]
dt_ck[, rv_ia_s  := zoo::rollmean(rv_ia,  k = k, fill = NA, align = "right")]

p1 <- ggplot(dt_ck, aes(x = t)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = rv_raw_s, colour = "Realised (smoothed)"),
            linewidth = 0.5, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_line(aes(y = v_raw, colour = "GARCH forecast"),
            linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("Realised (smoothed)" = "steelblue", "GARCH forecast" = "tomato")) +
  labs(title = "May OOS (Raw): smoothed r² vs GARCH σ²",
       x = "5-min bucket index", y = "Variance", colour = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p2 <- ggplot(dt_ck, aes(x = t)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = rv_ia_s, colour = "Realised (smoothed)"),
            linewidth = 0.5, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_line(aes(y = v_ia, colour = "GARCH forecast"),
            linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("Realised (smoothed)" = "steelblue", "GARCH forecast" = "tomato")) +
  labs(title = "May OOS (IA-sum): smoothed r² vs GARCH σ²",
       x = "5-min bucket index", y = "Variance", colour = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p1)
print(p2)
```

```{r nb06_vol_scores, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ── LOSS FUNCTIONS: QLIKE and MSE on variance ─────────────────────────────────
# QLIKE (Patton 2011) is the dominant loss function in the volatility forecasting
# literature because it is robust to noise in the realised proxy (here r²).
# It penalises over-prediction and under-prediction asymmetrically in a way
# that is consistent with the true (unobservable) variance as the target.
# MSE on variance is included as a standard complement.

score_vol <- function(r, sigma_hat, label) {
  rv   <- pmax(as.numeric(r)^2,         1e-12)
  vhat <- pmax(as.numeric(sigma_hat)^2, 1e-12)
  data.table(
    Sample  = label,
    MSE_Var = mean((rv - vhat)^2,         na.rm = TRUE),
    QLIKE   = mean(log(vhat) + rv / vhat, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

scores <- rbindlist(list(
  score_vol(r_may_raw_vol, sigma_raw_may, "May (Raw)"),
  score_vol(r_may_ia_vol,  sigma_ia_may,  "May (IA-sum)"),
  score_vol(r_jun_raw_vol, sigma_raw_jun, "Jun (Raw)"),
  score_vol(r_jun_ia_vol,  sigma_ia_jun,  "Jun (IA-sum)")
))
print(scores)

# Improvement table (positive % = IA better)
imp <- data.table(Month = c("May", "Jun"))
imp[, MSE_Raw   := c(scores[Sample == "May (Raw)",    MSE_Var],
                     scores[Sample == "Jun (Raw)",    MSE_Var])]
imp[, MSE_IA    := c(scores[Sample == "May (IA-sum)", MSE_Var],
                     scores[Sample == "Jun (IA-sum)", MSE_Var])]
imp[, QLIKE_Raw := c(scores[Sample == "May (Raw)",    QLIKE],
                     scores[Sample == "Jun (Raw)",    QLIKE])]
imp[, QLIKE_IA  := c(scores[Sample == "May (IA-sum)", QLIKE],
                     scores[Sample == "Jun (IA-sum)", QLIKE])]
imp[, MSE_Improvement_Pct   := 100 * (MSE_Raw   - MSE_IA)   / MSE_Raw]
imp[, QLIKE_Improvement_Pct := 100 * (QLIKE_Raw - QLIKE_IA) / abs(QLIKE_Raw)]

cat("\nIA improvement vs Raw (positive = IA better):\n")
print(imp[, .(Month, MSE_Raw, MSE_IA, MSE_Improvement_Pct,
              QLIKE_Raw, QLIKE_IA, QLIKE_Improvement_Pct)])
```

```{r nb06_mz_regression, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ── MINCER–ZARNOWITZ REGRESSION ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# Standard in the volatility forecasting literature (Hansen & Lunde 2005,
# Patton 2011). Regress realised proxy (r²) on forecast variance (σ²):
#   r²_t = α + β·σ²_t + ε_t
# Efficient forecast: α = 0, β = 1.
# HAC standard errors (Newey-West) correct for serial correlation in residuals.
# β < 1: model over-predicts variance spikes.
# β > 1: model under-predicts variance spikes.
# R²: how much variation in realised variance the forecast explains.

mz_test <- function(r, sigma_hat, label) {
  rv   <- as.numeric(r)^2
  vhat <- as.numeric(sigma_hat)^2

  fit <- lm(rv ~ vhat)
  L   <- floor(length(rv)^(1/3))
  V   <- NeweyWest(fit, lag = L, prewhite = FALSE, adjust = TRUE)
  ct  <- coeftest(fit, vcov. = V)

  alpha_hat <- ct[1, 1]; alpha_p <- ct[1, 4]
  beta_hat  <- ct[2, 1]; beta_p  <- ct[2, 4]
  r2        <- summary(fit)$r.squared

  # Joint Wald test H0: alpha=0, beta=1
  b <- coef(fit) - c(0, 1)
  W <- as.numeric(t(b) %*% solve(V) %*% b)
  p_joint <- 1 - pchisq(W, df = 2)

  data.table(
    Sample    = label,
    alpha_hat = round(alpha_hat, 8), alpha_p = round(alpha_p, 4),
    beta_hat  = round(beta_hat,  4), beta_p  = round(beta_p,  4),
    R2        = round(r2, 4),
    W_joint   = round(W, 3),         p_joint = round(p_joint, 4)
  )
}

mz <- rbindlist(list(
  mz_test(r_may_raw_vol, sigma_raw_may, "May (Raw)"),
  mz_test(r_may_ia_vol,  sigma_ia_may,  "May (IA-sum)"),
  mz_test(r_jun_raw_vol, sigma_raw_jun, "Jun (Raw)"),
  mz_test(r_jun_ia_vol,  sigma_ia_jun,  "Jun (IA-sum)")
))

cat("Mincer-Zarnowitz regression (HAC SEs):\n")
print(mz)
cat("\n  beta closer to 1 => better calibrated forecast\n")
cat("  p_joint < 0.05   => reject forecast efficiency (alpha=0, beta=1)\n")
```

```{r nb06_dm_hac_vol, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ── DIEBOLD–MARIANO TEST (HAC) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# d_t = Loss_raw(t) - Loss_ia(t)
# H1: mean(d) > 0  =>  IA-sum has lower loss than Raw (one-sided test).
# Newey-West HAC standard errors correct for serial correlation in d_t.
# HAC lag: T^(1/3) rule-of-thumb (Andrews 1991).

eps <- 1e-12

loss_series <- function(r, sigma_hat, loss = c("QLIKE", "MSE_Var")) {
  loss <- match.arg(loss)
  rv   <- pmax(as.numeric(r)^2,         eps)
  vhat <- pmax(as.numeric(sigma_hat)^2, eps)
  switch(loss,
    QLIKE   = log(vhat) + rv / vhat,
    MSE_Var = (rv - vhat)^2
  )
}

dm_hac <- function(d, L = NULL, alternative = c("greater", "two.sided")) {
  alternative <- match.arg(alternative)
  d <- as.numeric(na.omit(d))
  T <- length(d)
  stopifnot(T > 30)
  if (is.null(L)) L <- floor(T^(1/3))

  fit <- lm(d ~ 1)
  V   <- NeweyWest(fit, lag = L, prewhite = FALSE, adjust = TRUE)
  ct  <- coeftest(fit, vcov. = V)

  est   <- as.numeric(ct[1, 1])
  tstat <- as.numeric(ct[1, 3])
  pval  <- if (alternative == "greater") {
    1 - pt(tstat, df = T - 1)
  } else {
    2 * (1 - pt(abs(tstat), df = T - 1))
  }

  data.table(T = T, HAC_lag = L, mean_d = est, t_stat = tstat, p_value = pval)
}

# Loss differentials
d_may_qlike <- loss_series(r_may_raw_vol, sigma_raw_may, "QLIKE") -
               loss_series(r_may_ia_vol,  sigma_ia_may,  "QLIKE")
d_jun_qlike <- loss_series(r_jun_raw_vol, sigma_raw_jun, "QLIKE") -
               loss_series(r_jun_ia_vol,  sigma_ia_jun,  "QLIKE")
d_may_mse   <- loss_series(r_may_raw_vol, sigma_raw_may, "MSE_Var") -
               loss_series(r_may_ia_vol,  sigma_ia_may,  "MSE_Var")
d_jun_mse   <- loss_series(r_jun_raw_vol, sigma_raw_jun, "MSE_Var") -
               loss_series(r_jun_ia_vol,  sigma_ia_jun,  "MSE_Var")

dm_out <- rbindlist(list(
  cbind(Month = "May", Loss = "QLIKE",   dm_hac(d_may_qlike, alternative = "greater")),
  cbind(Month = "Jun", Loss = "QLIKE",   dm_hac(d_jun_qlike, alternative = "greater")),
  cbind(Month = "May", Loss = "MSE_Var", dm_hac(d_may_mse,   alternative = "greater")),
  cbind(Month = "Jun", Loss = "MSE_Var", dm_hac(d_jun_mse,   alternative = "greater"))
))
cat("DM-HAC tests (H1: IA-sum has lower loss than Raw):\n")
print(dm_out)

# Bucket-by-bucket win rate
win_rate <- function(d) {
  d <- as.numeric(na.omit(d))
  data.table(T = length(d), WinRate_IA = mean(d > 0), TieRate = mean(d == 0))
}

wins <- rbindlist(list(
  cbind(Month = "May", Loss = "QLIKE",   win_rate(d_may_qlike)),
  cbind(Month = "Jun", Loss = "QLIKE",   win_rate(d_jun_qlike)),
  cbind(Month = "May", Loss = "MSE_Var", win_rate(d_may_mse)),
  cbind(Month = "Jun", Loss = "MSE_Var", win_rate(d_jun_mse))
))
cat("\nDirectional win-rate (share of buckets where IA has lower loss):\n")
print(wins)
```

```{r nb06_robustness_ia_inv, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ── ROBUSTNESS: IA-invertible track as alternative volatility series ──────────
# IA-inv = r_raw_5m × scale_5m (invertible by construction, used in risk track).
# We rerun the same best spec on this track to confirm results are not sensitive
# to the choice of IA aggregation method (sum vs invertible).

r_apr_ia_inv <- as.numeric(r5_apr$r_ia_5m_inv)
r_may_ia_inv <- as.numeric(r5_may$r_ia_5m_inv)
r_jun_ia_inv <- as.numeric(r5_jun$r_ia_5m_inv)

stopifnot(all(is.finite(r_apr_ia_inv)),
          all(is.finite(r_may_ia_inv)),
          all(is.finite(r_jun_ia_inv)))

fit_ia_inv <- ugarchfit(spec_ia_best, data = r_apr_ia_inv, solver = "hybrid")
stopifnot(fit_ia_inv@fit$convergence == 0)

sigma_iaInv_may <- oos_sigma_fixed(fit_ia_inv, r_may_ia_inv)
sigma_iaInv_jun <- oos_sigma_fixed(fit_ia_inv, r_jun_ia_inv)

score_inv <- rbindlist(list(
  score_vol(r_may_ia_inv, sigma_iaInv_may, "May (IA-inv)"),
  score_vol(r_jun_ia_inv, sigma_iaInv_jun, "Jun (IA-inv)")
))

# Side-by-side with IA-sum scores
cmp <- rbindlist(list(
  scores[grepl("IA", Sample),
         .(Month = fifelse(grepl("May", Sample), "May", "Jun"),
           Source = "IA-sum", MSE_Var, QLIKE)],
  score_inv[,
         .(Month = fifelse(grepl("May", Sample), "May", "Jun"),
           Source = "IA-inv", MSE_Var, QLIKE)]
), use.names = TRUE)
setorder(cmp, Month, Source)

cat("Robustness: IA-sum vs IA-inv (lower is better):\n")
print(cmp)

ggplot(cmp, aes(x = Month, y = QLIKE, fill = Source)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Robustness: IA-sum vs IA-inv (QLIKE, lower is better)",
       x = NULL, y = "QLIKE", fill = "Aggregation method") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r nb06_headline, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ── HEADLINE TABLE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Build cleanly from the scores object using separate dcast calls to avoid
# column naming ambiguity from hyphenated level names.

sc <- copy(scores)
sc[, Month := fifelse(grepl("^May", Sample), "May", "Jun")]
sc[, Type  := fifelse(grepl("IA",  Sample), "IA",  "Raw")]

mse_w <- dcast(sc, Month ~ Type, value.var = "MSE_Var")
setnames(mse_w, c("IA", "Raw"), c("MSE_IA", "MSE_Raw"))

qlk_w <- dcast(sc, Month ~ Type, value.var = "QLIKE")
setnames(qlk_w, c("IA", "Raw"), c("QLIKE_IA", "QLIKE_Raw"))

vol_wide <- merge(mse_w, qlk_w, by = "Month")
vol_wide[, MSE_Improvement_Pct   := 100 * (MSE_Raw   - MSE_IA)   / MSE_Raw]
vol_wide[, QLIKE_Improvement_Pct := 100 * (QLIKE_Raw - QLIKE_IA) / abs(QLIKE_Raw)]
vol_wide[, Month := factor(Month, levels = c("May", "Jun"))]
setorder(vol_wide, Month)

# Attach DM test results
dm_q <- dm_out[Loss == "QLIKE",   .(Month, DM_t_QLIKE = t_stat, DM_p_QLIKE = p_value)]
dm_m <- dm_out[Loss == "MSE_Var", .(Month, DM_t_MSE   = t_stat, DM_p_MSE   = p_value)]
vol_headline <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "Month", all.x = TRUE),
                       list(vol_wide, dm_q, dm_m))
setorder(vol_headline, Month)

cat("\n=== VOLATILITY FORECAST HEADLINE (OOS May/Jun; lower loss = better) ===\n")
print(vol_headline[, .(
  Month,
  MSE_Raw    = format(MSE_Raw,  scientific = TRUE, digits = 3),
  MSE_IA     = format(MSE_IA,   scientific = TRUE, digits = 3),
  `MSE Δ%`   = round(MSE_Improvement_Pct,   2),
  QLIKE_Raw  = round(QLIKE_Raw,  5),
  QLIKE_IA   = round(QLIKE_IA,   5),
  `QLIKE Δ%` = round(QLIKE_Improvement_Pct, 3),
  DM_t_QLIKE = round(DM_t_QLIKE, 3),
  DM_p_QLIKE = round(DM_p_QLIKE, 4)
)])

# Plots
p_qlike <- ggplot(vol_headline, aes(x = Month, y = QLIKE_Improvement_Pct)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Volatility track: IA-sum vs Raw — QLIKE improvement (%)",
       x = NULL, y = "QLIKE improvement (%)  [positive = IA better]") +
  theme_minimal()

p_mse <- ggplot(vol_headline, aes(x = Month, y = MSE_Improvement_Pct)) +
  geom_col(fill = "tomato") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Volatility track: IA-sum vs Raw — MSE improvement (%)",
       x = NULL, y = "MSE improvement (%)  [positive = IA better]") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_qlike)
print(p_mse)
```

```{r nb06_save, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ── SAVE OUTPUTS for notebook 07 ─────────────────────────────────────────────
saveRDS(list(
  fit_raw_vol   = fit_raw_vol,
  fit_ia_vol    = fit_ia_vol,
  fit_ia_inv    = fit_ia_inv,
  spec_raw_best = spec_raw_best,
  spec_ia_best  = spec_ia_best,
  sigma_raw_may = sigma_raw_may,
  sigma_ia_may  = sigma_ia_may,
  sigma_raw_jun = sigma_raw_jun,
  sigma_ia_jun  = sigma_ia_jun,
  scores        = scores,
  dm_out        = dm_out,
  wins          = wins,
  vol_headline  = vol_headline
), file.path(proj_root, "data/processed/nb06_outputs.rds"))

cat("Saved: data/processed/nb06_outputs.rds\n")
cat("Contents: fit objects, sigma series, scores, dm_out, wins, vol_headline\n")
```
